title: About
name: Doug Fessler
headline: Senior Gameplay Engineer
bio: I'm a Senior Gameplay Engineer at Blizzard Entertainment, currently working on an unannounced project. I spend most of my time on the boundary between design and the engine: mission systems, in-game cinematics, save architecture, quest logic. The goal is always the same: build tools that let designers author and debug their own work independently.

I got into this through RPG Maker. Spent a lot of weekends making it do things it wasn't designed for, which eventually pushed me to learn C++ so I could go further. That led to FireForge, shipping Ghostbusters and a couple of prototypes, then Blizzard where I worked on Hearthstone for three years across five expansions. I've been on teams of three and teams of over 300, including contract studios overseas, and genuinely enjoyed both.

stats.label: focus
stats.value: Mission Systems / Open World / Gameplay / IGC

highlights.title: Last Sentinel
highlights.description: Mission systems, IGC framework, and cinematic tooling for a narrative open-world action game.

highlights.title: Hearthstone
highlights.description: Solo adventures, journal systems, quests, and achievements across five major expansions.

highlights.title: Ghostbusters
highlights.description: AI and gameplay ability systems on the official Ghostbusters tie-in for PC, PS4, and Xbox One.
